title: Earnings: As of December 31, 2026
content:
Net Income Year-to-Date totaled $1,712K for the credit union, supported primarily by Net Interest Income of $7,803K. This spread — derived from Interest Income of $10,792K against Interest Expense of $2,989K — remains the dominant contributor to profitability.

Non-Interest Income Year-to-Date amounted to $2,608K and served as a meaningful supplement to core earnings. Fee income was the largest component at $1,380K, followed by Other Non-Interest Income of $1,217K; Gains (Loss) on Dispositions contributed only $19K.

Non-Interest Expense Year-to-Date totaled $8,359K. The expense base is heavily concentrated in Credit Loss Expense at $340K and Operating Fees (Examina...) at $4,417K; Office Occupancy Expense ($853K) and Travel/Conference Expenses ($262K) are secondary items.

Overall Position: Net income of $1,712K resulted from the spread between interest income and expense being offset by non-interest expenses that exceeded total revenue. Sustaining profitability will depend on maintaining fee generation at current levels while controlling credit loss exposure.
